en: My brothers, show no partiality as you hold the faith in our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory.
zh: 我的弟兄們，你們既然對我們榮耀的主耶穌基督有信心，就不應該憑外貌待人。

en: For if a man wearing a gold ring and fine clothing comes into your assembly, and a poor man in shabby clothing also comes in,
zh: 如果有一個手戴金戒指、身穿華麗衣服的人，進入你們的會堂；又有一個衣衫襤褸的窮人，也進去了。

en: and if you pay attention to the one who wears the fine clothing and say, "You sit here in a good place," while you say to the poor man, "You stand over there," or, "Sit down at my feet,"
zh: 你們就看重那穿華麗衣服的人，說：“請坐在這好位上。”又對那個窮人說：“你站在那裡。”或說：“坐在我的腳凳下邊。”

en: have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts?
zh: 這不是你們對人有歧視，成了心懷惡意的審判官嗎？

en: Listen, my beloved brothers, has not God chosen those who are poor in the world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom, which he has promised to those who love him?
zh: 我親愛的弟兄們，請聽： 神不是揀選了在世上被認為貧窮的人嗎？這些人卻在信心上富足，而且是承受 神的國的人。這國是 神應許賜給愛他的人的。

en: But you have dishonored the poor man. Are not the rich the ones who oppress you, and the ones who drag you into court?
zh: 然而你們卻侮辱窮人。其實，那些欺壓你們，拉你們上法庭的，不就是富足的人嗎？

en: Are they not the ones who blaspheme the honorable name by which you were called?
zh: 難道不是他們褻瀆那召你們的尊名嗎？

en: If you really fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, "You shall love your neighbor as yourself," you are doing well.
zh: 你們若照著聖經所說“要愛人如己”這話，去完成這至尊的律法，你們就作對了。

en: But if you show partiality, you are committing sin and are convicted by the law as transgressors.
zh: 如果你們憑外貌待人，就是犯罪，律法就要裁定你們是犯法的。

en: For whoever keeps the whole law but fails in one point has become accountable for all of it.
zh: 因為凡是遵守全部律法的，只要在一條上失足，就違犯所有的了。

en: For he who said, "Do not commit adultery," also said, "Do not murder." If you do not commit adultery but do murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.
zh: 就像那說“不可姦淫”的，也說“不可殺人”；你縱然不姦淫，卻殺人，還是犯法的。

en: So speak and so act as those who are to be judged under the law of liberty.
zh: 你們既然按著使人自由的律法受審判，就應照著這律法說話行事。

en: For judgment is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
zh: 因為對不行憐憫的人，審判他們的時候就沒有憐憫；憐憫勝過審判。

en: What good is it, my brothers, if someone says he has faith but does not have works? Can that faith save him?
zh: 我的弟兄們，人若說他有信心，卻沒有行為，有甚麼益處呢？這信心能救他嗎？

en: If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food,
zh: 如果有弟兄或姊妹缺衣少食，

en: and one of you says to them, "Go in peace, be warmed and filled," without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that?
zh: 而你們中間有人對他們說：“平平安安地去吧！願你們穿得暖，吃得飽。”卻不給他們身體所需用的，那有甚麼用處呢？

en: So also faith by itself, if it does not have works, is dead.
zh: 照樣，如果只有信心，沒有行為，這信心就是死的。

en: But someone will say, "You have faith and I have works." Show me your faith apart from your works, and I will show you my faith by my works.
zh: 也許有人要說，你有信心，我有行為；請把你沒有行為的信心指給我看，我就藉著我的行為，把我的信心指給你看。

en: You believe that God is one; you do well. Even the demons believe- and shudder!
zh: 你信 神只有一位，你信的不錯；就連鬼魔也信，卻是戰兢。

en: Do you want to be shown, you foolish person, that faith apart from works is useless?
zh: 愚昧的人哪，你願意知道沒有行為的信心是沒有用的嗎？

en: Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered up his son Isaac on the altar?
zh: 我們的祖先亞伯拉罕，把他的兒子以撒獻在祭壇上，不是因行為稱義嗎？

en: You see that faith was active along with his works, and faith was completed by his works;
zh: 你看，他的信心與行為是一致的，信心就因著行為得到完全了；

en: and the Scripture was fulfilled that says, "Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness"- and he was called a friend of God.
zh: 這正應驗了經上所說的：“亞伯拉罕信 神，這就算為他的義。”他也被稱為 神的朋友。

en: You see that a person is justified by works and not by faith alone.
zh: 可見人稱義是因著行為，不僅是因著信心。

en: And in the same way was not also Rahab the prostitute justified by works when she received the messengers and sent them out by another way?
zh: 照樣，妓女喇合接待了探子，又從另一條路把他們送走，不也是因行為稱義嗎？

en: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, so also faith apart from works is dead.
zh: 身體沒有靈魂是死的，照樣，信心沒有行為也是死的。
